title: Reposition An AGV To The Hospital Using Go-to During A Tipper-Down Recovery Example
summary: Use the documented Go-to recovery example to remove and add an AGV, send it to the hospital, and allow it to resume tasking after tote removal.
when_to_use: Use this source-described recovery example when a tipper goes down and an AGV will not leave, and the operator/support user needs to use the Robot Selection Go-to function to drop the AGV off at the hospital.
not_for: Do not use as a general-purpose AGV recovery procedure outside the specific source example of a tipper-down condition where an AGV will not leave.
Do not use when the source-required remove-and-add action cannot be performed or is not permitted.
Do not use as a substitute for procedures that define what to do if the AGV does not move to the selected square or does not pick up a new task after tote removal.

responsibilities: Perform the remove-and-add action on the AGV as referenced by the training material.
Use the Robot Selection Go-to function to send the AGV to the selected hospital square.
Remove the tote from the AGV and verify whether it picks up a new task.

safety_notes: The source presents this as a quick recovery example and does not define full risk boundaries or fallback controls.
Do not assume additional movement, floor-entry, or manual handling steps beyond those explicitly stated in the source.
Escalate if the AGV does not move to the selected square or does not pick up a new task after tote removal.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the Robot Selection Go-to function
Ability to remove and add the AGV
Ability to remove the tote from the AGV

access_required: Access to the Robot Selection Go-to function
Permission or ability to remove and add the AGV
system_preconditions: A tipper-down recovery condition exists where an AGV will not leave.
The AGV is identified for the recovery example.
A hospital drop-off square is available to be selected in Go-to.

visual_references.description: Training slide frame showing the Robot Selection Go-to function and the recovery example for sending an AGV to the hospital.

steps.title: Identify the AGV for the tipper-down recovery example
steps.purpose: Ensure the correct AGV is selected for the documented recovery example.
steps.instruction: Identify the AGV involved in the recovery example where a tipper goes down and the AGV will not leave.
steps.expected_result: The AGV to be recovered is identified for Go-to handling.
steps.healthy_condition: The correct AGV is identified as the one that will not leave during the tipper-down condition.
steps.failure_condition: The affected AGV cannot be clearly identified from the current recovery situation.

actions.label: Identify affected AGV
actions.exact_action: Identify the AGV involved in the recovery example where a tipper goes down and the AGV will not leave.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The slide text describing the recovery example: a tipper goes down and an AGV will not leave.

stop_or_escalate_if: The affected AGV cannot be confidently identified.
The situation does not match the source example of a tipper-down condition where an AGV will not leave.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Slide example: if a tipper goes down and an AGV will not leave, the Go-to function is helpful for quick recovery and can be used to drop it off at the hospital.

steps.title: Remove and add the AGV
steps.purpose: Apply the source-stated prerequisite recovery action before using Go-to.
steps.instruction: Remove and add the AGV, as stated on the training slide.
steps.expected_result: The AGV has been removed and added back in preparation for Go-to recovery.
steps.healthy_condition: The AGV has been successfully removed and added back as part of the recovery example.
steps.failure_condition: The AGV cannot be removed and added, or the source-described action does not complete successfully.
stop_or_escalate_if: The source does not provide a fallback if remove and add does not work.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The slide describes a recovery workflow for when a tipper goes down and an AGV will not leave: remove and add the AGV, use the Go to function, drop it off at the hospital, and the AGV will pick up a new task once its tote has been removed.
Slide content shows Robot Selection with Add Robot and Remove Robot procedures in the web application: enter the corresponding robot ID, click Add or Remove, and verify the system reports success.

steps.title: Select the AGV in Robot Selection Go-to
steps.purpose: Choose the AGV that will be sent to the hospital using the Go-to function.
steps.instruction: Select the AGV in the Robot Selection Go-to function.
steps.expected_result: The target AGV is selected in Robot Selection.
steps.healthy_condition: The intended AGV is selected and ready for the Go-to action.
steps.failure_condition: The AGV cannot be selected or the wrong AGV is selected.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The Robot Selection Go-to function and the listed step to select an AGV.

stop_or_escalate_if: The AGV cannot be selected in Robot Selection.
There is uncertainty about whether the selected AGV is the affected AGV.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Steps shown are select an AGV, select Go to, select which square you want the AGV to go to, and select Confirm.

steps.title: Select Go to
steps.purpose: Initiate the Go-to action for the selected AGV.
steps.expected_result: The Go-to action is opened for the selected AGV.
steps.healthy_condition: The Go-to function is active for the selected AGV.
steps.failure_condition: The Go-to function cannot be selected or does not open for the AGV.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The Go-to step in the Robot Selection workflow.

stop_or_escalate_if: Go-to is unavailable for the selected AGV.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Steps shown are select an AGV, select Go to, select which square you want the AGV to go to, and select Confirm.

steps.title: Select the hospital square
steps.purpose: Choose the destination square used to drop the AGV off at the hospital.
steps.instruction: Select the square used to drop the AGV off at the hospital.
steps.expected_result: A hospital destination square is selected for the AGV.
steps.healthy_condition: The intended hospital square is selected as the AGV destination.
steps.failure_condition: A hospital square cannot be selected or the destination is unclear.

actions.label: Choose destination square
actions.exact_action: Select which square you want the AGV to go to.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The step to select which square the AGV should go to, in the context of dropping it off at the hospital.
The training frame describing hospital flow and hospital drop-off context.

stop_or_escalate_if: The correct hospital square is not known from the current source context.
The AGV destination cannot be selected.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The slide describes a recovery workflow for when a tipper goes down and an AGV will not leave: remove and add the AGV, use the Go to function, drop it off at the hospital.
The hospital is used as a home base, for reject handling when an AGV cannot tip, for initializing new AGVs, and for shutdown-state behavior when AGVs are removed or added.

steps.title: Confirm the Go-to destination
steps.purpose: Submit the selected hospital destination for the AGV.
steps.expected_result: The Go-to request is confirmed for the selected hospital square.
steps.healthy_condition: The destination selection is confirmed and the AGV is expected to move to the selected square.
steps.failure_condition: The confirmation cannot be completed or the AGV does not proceed toward the selected square.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The final Confirm step in the Go-to workflow.

stop_or_escalate_if: The AGV does not move to the selected square after confirmation.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Steps shown are select an AGV, select Go to, select which square you want the AGV to go to, and select Confirm.

steps.title: Remove the tote from the AGV
steps.purpose: Complete the source-described condition that allows the AGV to pick up a new task.
steps.instruction: Remove the tote from the AGV.
steps.expected_result: The tote is removed from the AGV.
steps.healthy_condition: The AGV no longer has the tote that was preventing normal task progression in this example.
steps.failure_condition: The tote cannot be removed or remains on the AGV.

actions.label: Remove tote
actions.exact_action: Remove the tote from the AGV.

stop_or_escalate_if: The tote cannot be removed from the AGV.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The AGV will pick up a new task once its tote has been removed.

steps.title: Verify the AGV picks up a new task
steps.purpose: Confirm the recovery example has succeeded after tote removal.
steps.instruction: Verify that the AGV picks up a new task once its tote has been removed.
steps.expected_result: The AGV picks up a new task after tote removal.
steps.healthy_condition: The AGV resumes tasking after being dropped at the hospital and having its tote removed.
steps.failure_condition: The AGV does not pick up a new task after tote removal.

actions.label: Verify new task
actions.exact_action: Verify that the AGV picks up a new task once its tote has been removed.

stop_or_escalate_if: The AGV does not pick up a new task after tote removal.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The AGV will pick up a new task once its tote has been removed.

success_criteria: The AGV is removed and added as described in the source example.
The AGV is sent to the selected hospital square using Go-to.
The tote is removed from the AGV.
The AGV picks up a new task after tote removal.
healthy_conditions: The correct AGV can be identified and selected.
The Go-to workflow can be completed through Confirm.
The AGV moves to the selected hospital square.
The AGV picks up a new task after its tote is removed.
failure_conditions: The AGV cannot be removed and added.
The AGV cannot be selected in Robot Selection Go-to.
The hospital square cannot be selected or confirmed.
The AGV does not move to the selected square.
The tote cannot be removed from the AGV.
The AGV does not pick up a new task after tote removal.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if remove and add does not work, because the source does not define the next action.
Escalate if the AGV does not move to the selected square.
Escalate if the AGV does not pick up a new task after tote removal.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Robot Selection Go-to function slide and the recovery example steps.
Hospital flow context and hospital drop-off concept.

missing_details: The source does not define the exact UI path or screen navigation for opening Robot Selection Go-to.
The source does not define the exact method for identifying the correct hospital square beyond selecting a square.
The source does not define what to do if remove and add does not work.
The source does not define timing expectations for AGV movement or task pickup.
The source does not define role boundaries, permissions, or safety controls for tote removal.
